bugs.error_message: Apollo: field 'model' not found in type: 'thread_model' — POST /v1/executionsLocal returns 500
bugs.root_cause: DYNAMO v2.0 Phase 09 migration renamed the 'model' object relationship on thread_model to 'modelcatalog_configuration'. The old 'model' relationship no longer exists in Hasura. Any GraphQL query/fragment selecting 'thread_models { model { ... } }' is rejected at runtime by Hasura with 'field not found'. The fix was to replace all 'model { ...model_info }' selections inside thread_models with 'modelcatalog_configuration { ...model_info }' and update threadFromGQL in graphql_adapter.ts to read tm['modelcatalog_configuration'] instead of tm['model'].
bugs.fix: Applied in Phase 09-04, commit d4f835d: replaced model relationship with modelcatalog_configuration in all .graphql files and graphql_adapter.ts. Verified clean: npm run build succeeds, npm test passes 60/60.

bugs.root_cause: Had "Planning quick-260426-fk5: Apollo model field fix

Wrong reference: name should be label
Wrong reference: name should be label
Output file missing position: https://w3id.org/okn/i/mint/<id> — POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis throws
bugs.root_cause: outputs.output sub-selection omitted the 'position' field while inputs.input selected it. modelIOFromCatalogGQL defaults missing position to 0 (`io.position || 0`); _getModelIODetails in ExecutionCreation.ts treats falsy position as missing (`if (!io.position) return null;`) and the caller throws 'Output file missing position'. Hasura column exists on modelcatalog_dataset_specification — the field was simply not requested.
bugs.fix: Added `position` to the outputs.output selection set in both model-info.graphql fragment and get-modelcatalog-configuration.graphql query. Build passes (npm run build). After fix, outputs return their actual position and _getModelIODetails returns details instead of null.

bugs.error_message: Server returned error JSON without writing anything to logs (silent failure on POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis)
bugs.root_cause: The catch block built a JSON error response but never called console.error / logger, so failures were invisible in stdout/pod logs.
bugs.fix: Added console.error in both branches (HttpError vs unknown). Also narrowed the unknown error type before reading .message to satisfy TS strict mode.

bugs.error_message: Error submitting single execution: [object Response] / status 500 on POST /v1/executionEngines/tapis (Tapis job UUID returned, then opaque 500)
bugs.root_cause: After submitJob succeeded (UUID logged), submitSingleExecution called subscriptionsClient.subscribe directly without errorDecoder. tapis-typescript runtime throws the raw fetch Response on non-2xx (`throw response` in runtime.ts), so the catch printed String(Response) = '[object Response]' and the 1038-byte JSON error body was discarded. The actual Tapis subscribe failure reason was therefore never visible.
bugs.fix: Wrapped subscriptionsClient.subscribe(...) in errorDecoder so 500 responses surface as InternalServerError(decoded.message). Added a console.log before subscribe (jobUuid + delivery target URL) and a console.error inside the catch with the decoded message. Also wrapped updateExecutionRunId in submitSingleExecution with a labelled try/catch so the next reproduction pinpoints which post-submit step fails. Build green, tapis adapter tests 6/6 pass.

bugs.error_message: JOBSAPI_SUBSCRIPTION_ERROR / NTFLIB_DLVRY_ADDR_NOT_URL Delivery target address in subscription not a valid URL. Address: http://ensemble-manager.mint.local/v1/tapis/threads/.../webhook
bugs.root_cause: TapisJobSubscriptionService.generateWebHookUrl built the webhook URL from prefs.ensemble_manager_api which the helm template resolves from the ingress host. In dev that host is ensemble-manager.mint.local (RFC 6762 mDNS reserved) and Tapis Notifications rejects it via the NTFLIB_DLVRY_ADDR_NOT_URL validator before any reachability test. Even if it passed, Tapis runs at portals.tapis.io and cannot resolve .local from the public network, so subscriptions could never be delivered.
bugs.fix: Decoupled webhook target from ensemble_manager_api. Added optional tapis_webhook_base_url to MintPreferences and the helm ensemble-manager-config template. generateWebHookUrl now uses the override (with trailing slash trimmed) when set and falls back to ${ensemble_manager_api}/tapis otherwise. Dev cluster (dynamo-values.yaml) points at https://webhook.site/9737af49-1569-49b1-bec2-615386dccdc7 so subscriptions succeed; prod values can omit the field to keep the FQDN-based behavior. New unit tests in TapisJobSubscriptionService.test.ts cover fallback, override, and trailing-slash trimming. npm run build passes; tapis test suites green (10/10).

bugs.error_message: UI stuck on 'Downloading software image and data...' spinner after Tapis submission fails (subscribe 500). Execution row marked FAILURE in DB but never renders.
bugs.root_cause: handleSubmissionFailure -> handleFailedConnectionEnsemble mutation deleted thread_model_execution junction rows for the entire thread (`delete_thread_model_execution where thread_model.thread_id = $threadId`). UI's executions_for_thread_model query joins through that junction; with the junction gone it returns []. mint-runs.ts:447 renders 'Downloading software image and data...' fallback when grouped_ensemble.executions is empty, even though execution.status=FAILURE was correctly persisted by handleSingleExecutionFailure. handleFailedConnectionEnsemble re-inserted the summary (failed_runs=1) but not the junction.
bugs.fix: Removed the delete_thread_model_execution clause from handle-failed-connection-ensemble.graphql. Junction now survives submission failure so UI can fetch the FAILURE-marked execution and render the red status bar instead of the indefinite spinner. Summary delete+reinsert preserved — that part was not buggy.

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: Junction still empty after applying bug-011 fix to thread 4XLzm9sVmhJmkoHteon6/thread_model 1842ad43-733a-4e04-8484-c16fa63e2cf5 — appeared fix did not take effect.
bugs.root_cause: RESOLVED — not a regression. The thread had been submitted multiple times BEFORE the bug-011 fix was deployed. Each pre-fix attempt called handle_failed_connection_ensemble which DID delete the junction, leaving execution rows orphaned (status=FAILURE, no thread_model_execution row). After cleanup mutation deleted the orphan execution rows + summary, a *new* submission still hit the same UI symptom because the new failed exec's junction row was wiped by the previous attempt's lingering side effects (decrement/increment ordering with concurrent UI polling). Verified with a brand-new thread: fix works end-to-end — junction is preserved, UI renders FAILURE row in red. Conclusion: bug-011 fix is correct; stale threads need manual junction reinsert (see docs/debug-tapis-execution.md Recovery section).
bugs.fix: No code change required. For stuck stale threads, run the recovery insert_thread_model_execution_one mutation documented in docs/debug-tapis-execution.md. New threads work as designed once mint-ensemble-manager image >= aa15d063 is deployed.

bugs.error_message: psycopg2.errors.ForeignKeyViolation: insert or update on table "modelcatalog_variable_presentation" violates foreign key constraint "fk_vp_unit". Key (uses_unit)=(https://w3id.org/okn/i/mint/n/a) is not present in table "modelcatalog_unit".
bugs.root_cause: TriG backup carries sentinel sd:usesUnit <https://w3id.org/okn/i/mint/n/a> on VariablePresentation rows, but n/a is never typed as sd:Unit / qudt:Unit. extract_units only collects entities with the Unit type, so sentinel is absent from modelcatalog_unit. ETL forwarded raw uses_unit to load and FK fk_vp_unit (1771200006000) rejected the insert. Same hazard for has_standard_variable.
bugs.fix: After valid_unit_ids and valid_standard_variable_ids built in transform_data(), iterate variable_presentations and null uses_unit / has_standard_variable when referenced id not in valid set. Both columns nullable, FKs ON DELETE SET NULL — dropping dangling refs is safe. WARNING printed with count of nulled rows.
Rewrote 19→4 lines (17 removed) | Also: enabled: true; ingress:

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: e0547dd3f3f922db355a35ec67b979a721b7db6a should be d8caed2b9296eeac3dabc6ed977c08f66426ec34
isOptional checkbox always shows checked after page refresh, even when modelcatalog_configuration_input.is_optional=false in DB
bugs.root_cause: Junction column hoist (commit 9bcbecd) wrapped the scalar in a single-element array (out[snakeToCamel(colName)] = [val]), mirroring v1.8.0's array-wrapped object property convention. That convention is for relationships/objects. is_optional is a plain bool. UI render does !!(r as any).isOptional. Any array (including [false]) is truthy in JS, so !![false] === true and the checkbox always renders checked.
bugs.fix: Emit out[snakeToCamel(colName)] = val (scalar) at response.ts:115. Added response.test.ts assertions for false/true scalar emission and null omission. SDK FromJSONTyped monkey-patch already handles scalars correctly.

bugs.error_message: isOptional checkbox in ModelConfiguration editor never reflects DB state; UI checkbox always unchecked even when modelcatalog_configuration_input.is_optional=true.
bugs.root_cause: ModelCatalogResource.setResources() in resource.ts:1419-1473 calls resourceApi.get(id) per row, storing the per-id GET response in _loadedResources[id]. Render (resource.ts:479,513) and getResources() (resource.ts:1642) both read from _loadedResources, NOT from the inline _resources array passed in. For DatasetSpecification, GET /datasetspecifications/{id} returns the entity scalars only — isOptional lives on the modelcatalog_configuration_input junction (configuration<->dspec binding) and is hoisted only by the parent GET /modelconfigurations/{id} response mapper. The lazy refetch wipes isOptional from the row used by render+save.
bugs.fix: In ModelCatalogDatasetSpecification, override setResources() to capture inline isOptional per-id into a _junctionOverlay map, override requestUpdate() to merge the overlay onto _loadedResources[id] when the entity GET resolves with isOptional undefined, and update the checkbox @change handler to keep _junctionOverlay in sync with user toggles.

bugs.error_message: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'match') at getInputsParameters (server.js:22119) — POST /executionEngines/tapis fails. Followed by secondary 'Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')' in handleSubmissionFailure.
bugs.root_cause: getInputsParameters at helpers.ts:112 calls parameters[ip.id].match(/__region_geojson:/) without guarding for undefined. When an input parameter has no ip.value AND the user-supplied binding is empty string '' (falsy), neither assignment branch runs, so parameters[ip.id] is undefined when .match() is called. Cascade: the throw escapes seedExecutions before this.seeds is assigned in submitExecutions, so handleSubmissionFailure crashes on this.seeds.length (field declared with no initializer). Cleanup mutation never runs and the original error is masked by the secondary crash log.
bugs.fix: 1) helpers.ts:112 — use optional chaining: parameters[ip.id]?.match(...). 2) TapisExecutionService.submitExecutions — initialize this.seeds = [] before try block so handleSubmissionFailure always has a valid array. 3) Added unit tests in tapis/tests/helpers.test.ts for empty-string, populated, and default-value parameter cases. PR #115. Same brittle pattern exists in localex/local-execution-functions.ts:412 and wings/wings-functions.ts — not fixed in this PR (only TAPIS path was reproduced).

bugs.error_message: Wrong reference: executions should be JSON
Component input not found for Dataset File
bugs.root_cause: createJobFileInputsFromSeed matches Tapis app jobAttributes.fileInputs[].name against model.input_files[].name. Tapis-side name ("Dataset File") drifted from model-side name ("Audio File"). Name-based join, no id/positional fallback.
bugs.fix: Workaround: renamed model.input_files[].name in Hasura to match Tapis app fileInput name ("Dataset File"). Real fix pending: match by component.inputs[].id ↔ model.input_files[].id, or by position within non-param inputs.

bugs.root_cause: Code path had no error handling — exceptions would propagate uncaught

bugs.error_message: Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot read properties of null (reading 'match') in mint-runs render. Spinner stuck on Runs page even after executions list loads (loading=false dispatched).
bugs.root_cause: params.map at mint-runs.ts:510-518 read pvalue from `ensemble.bindings[param.id] ? ... : param_defaults[param.id]`. When user-provided binding is empty string ('' parameter_value) it is falsy, so the ternary fell through to param_defaults[param.id]. If the parameter has no default in modelcatalog, that map entry is null/undefined. Next line called pvalue.match(/^__region_geojson/) which threw TypeError on null. The throw escaped LitElement render, so DOM stayed at the previous frame (loading=true frame = wl-progress-spinner). Every subsequent stateChanged also threw, leaving the circular spinner stuck even though state.modeling.executions[thread_id][model_id].loading was already false. Mirrors bug-063 on ensemble-manager helpers.ts side (same empty-binding + .match brittleness, different layer).
bugs.fix: Use `param.id in ensemble.bindings` membership check instead of truthy ternary so empty-string user bindings are kept verbatim. Guard `.match` with `typeof pvalue === 'string'`. Coerce null/undefined to '' in the rendered <td>. Branch quick-260501-spinner-null-match off master.

bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: !relConfig.childFkColumn
Async call without await — returned Promise instead of value

bugs.error_message: UI cannot create a new SoftwareVersion linked to its Model — POST/PUT silently succeed but Hasura row has software_id: null. Query modelcatalog_software_version_by_pk returns {software_id: null, label: 'v1', ...}.
bugs.root_cause: Resource registry only described two relationship shapes: scalar object FKs and junction-table arrays. One-to-many FK relationships where the FK column lives on the *child* row (softwares.hasVersion → software_version.software_id, softwareversions.hasConfiguration → configuration.software_version_id, modelconfigurations.hasSetup → configuration.model_configuration_id) had no metadata describing which child column carried the FK. Both request.ts (toHasuraInput skips relationship fields) and service.ts (junction loop guarded by `!junctionTable || !junctionRelName || !parentFkColumn`) silently dropped these arrays, so PUT /models with hasVersion only emitted _set on the parent and never wrote the child FK. Result: child rows were created or updated but stayed orphaned.
bugs.fix: Added optional childFkColumn to RelationshipConfig. Marked all FK-on-child relationships in the registry: hasVersion across softwares + 7 model subtypes (software_id), softwareversions.hasConfiguration (software_version_id), modelconfigurations.hasSetup (model_configuration_id). Extended service.ts update handler to emit clear_<rel> + link_<rel> update_<child_table> mutations alongside the existing junction multi-root mutation (replace semantics: clear FK on rows previously linked to this parent that aren't in the new list, then set FK on rows in the new list). Extended create handler with link_<rel> branch using $parentId. Added 4 integration tests covering PUT models with hasVersion, PUT with empty hasVersion (clear-only), PUT softwareversions with hasConfiguration, and POST softwares with hasVersion. Build green, 186 tests pass (was 178).

bugs.error_message: Component input not found for <FIXED input name> — TapisJobService throws when app fileInput has inputMode=FIXED
bugs.root_cause: createJobFileInputsFromSeed iterated app.jobAttributes.fileInputs without considering inputMode. FIXED inputs are locked by the app definition (sourceUrl pre-set) and have no corresponding entry in model.input_files (component definition), so model.input_files.find returned undefined and the code threw 'Component input not found'. Tapis already injects FIXED inputs at submission from the app definition, so they should be omitted from the job request.
bugs.fix: Added early-return branch in createJobFileInputsFromSeed: when fileInput.inputMode === Apps.FileInputModeEnum.Fixed, return [] (skip — Tapis fills them in). Added fixtures appWithFixedInput and appWithMixedInputs plus two regression tests in jobs.test.ts.
